description_long: This is a sturdy, square-framed stool constructed from thick, rectangular wooden beams with a uniform brown finish. The stool features four straight legs, each connected at the top and bottom by horizontal supports, forming a rigid and stable structure. Diagonal cross-braces on each side add both strength and a geometric visual interest, intersecting the lower horizontal supports. The seat area is open, lacking a solid platform, which gives the stool a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible decorative elements or upholstery. Its robust build suggests it is suitable for outdoor or public urban environments, such as parks or sidewalks.
description: A robust, square-framed brown wooden stool with four legs and diagonal cross-braces, featuring an open seat and a minimalist, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square frame, four legs, and prominent diagonal cross-brace on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the parallel diagonal cross-braces and the open seat area.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the square frame and the intersecting cross-braces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the diagonal cross-brace on the right and the open seat structure.
